page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-64GB is a KVM-hypervised virtual server featuring 32 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, configured with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The underlying hardware utilizes an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz with an x86_64 architecture. This configuration allocates 32 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core on a shared CPU model. The processor includes 64 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache, alongside hardware support for AVX, AES, SHA_NI, and BMI2 instruction sets. No local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data indicates that the instance delivers average multi-core performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the bottom 10%. Conversely, the server demonstrates strong capabilities in specific parallelized tasks, scoring in the top 25% for multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, PassMark CPU extended instructions, and PassMark CPU encryption tests. Memory bandwidth remains average, with read-only large block speeds at 43.75 GB/sec and write large block speeds at 30.3 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation and high core density, this server type offers a cost-efficient profile for highly parallelized workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated single-core performance. It is suited for multi-threaded decompression, batch processing, encryption tasks, and moderate web serving. It is less suitable for single-threaded applications or large-scale LLM inference, where prompt processing for 70B models falls into the bottom 25%.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-64GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 32 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM on a shared CPU allocation model. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, this x86_64 instance features a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and lacks local storage. Performance benchmarks show a distinct tradeoff: single-core performance is poor, ranking in the bottom 10%, while multi-threaded capabilities are strong, with top 25% marks in bzip3 decompression, CPU extended instructions, and encryption tests. General multi-core, memory bandwidth, and web serving metrics fall within the average range. This server is suited for parallelized batch processing, multi-threaded decompression, and moderate web serving workloads where high core density is required without the cost of dedicated CPU cores.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-64GB is a KVM virtual server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and shared CPU allocation, offering strong multi-threaded decompression and encryption performance.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-64GB is a KVM virtual server featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and shared CPU allocation, designed for parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density, shared-CPU virtual server delivering strong multi-threaded performance and encryption capabilities for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 32 AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM on a shared CPU allocation
Strong multi-threaded performance in bzip3 decompression and encryption
Poor single-core CPU performance ranking in the bottom 10%
No bundled local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses
Suited for parallelized batch processing and moderate web serving
